name: North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center
anchor_hook: A historic schoolhouse turned community cultural center that keeps the Ridge visible inside Nevada County's arts map.
why_it_matters: It broadens the prototype beyond downtown venues and helps prove that cultural discovery includes rural gathering places, community memory, performance, and place-based identity.
image_status: real existing V1 image; verify asset path before implementation
photo_wish: Exterior or gathering image that shows the schoolhouse as a living community venue.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official North Columbia Schoolhouse site; evidence-backed: docs/CULTURAL-ANCHOR-SET-PROPOSAL.md; drafted-from-current-data: MUSE 2026 launch/Rural Is the New Cool context; drafted-from-current-data: Culture Forward listening-session context
path_membership: Ridge / rural culture path; community gathering path
muse_connection: MUSE launch/Rural Is the New Cool context makes this one of the clearest rural-culture anchors.
culture_forward_connection: Culture Forward listening-session context supports treating it as a community voice and geography anchor.
events_connection: Likely events/programming relevance; verify current public calendar before app copy.
visitor_use: Find a meaningful rural cultural destination and understand that culture is not only downtown.
stakeholder_use: Shows reviewers that the prototype can represent countywide cultural geography, not just convenience stops.
geo_role: Extends the map north/east into Ridge identity and prevents a downtown-only spine.
open_questions: needs-user-review: confirm exact Arts Council language and whether it belongs in the first V1 public route set.
demo_decision: Keep in data, but do not make it a top demo pin unless showing the Ridge/rural layer.
decision_basis: Perplexity and local context support it as a meaningful rural cultural node, but it is geographically peripheral to a tight GV/NC demo.

name: The Center for the Arts
anchor_hook: Grass Valley's major arts venue gives the map an immediate reason to plan around performances, exhibitions, classes, and community events.
why_it_matters: It is the clearest institutional arts anchor in Grass Valley and gives the prototype a strong public-discovery and stakeholder-proof spine.
photo_wish: Exterior first; theater or gallery alternate if the card should emphasize programming over wayfinding.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official Center for the Arts site; evidence-backed: official CFTA image candidates; evidence-backed: docs/anchor-cards/anchor-photo-candidates.csv; drafted-from-current-data: Culture Forward listening-session and MUSE launch context
path_membership: Living Like a Local; Evening Arts Night; performance-first path
muse_connection: MUSE 2025 launch/Be Many and Be Loud context supports public arts visibility.
culture_forward_connection: Culture Forward listening-session site/context supports it as stakeholder-recognized infrastructure.
events_connection: Strong current events/programming role; use official calendar if wiring live events later.
visitor_use: See what is happening now in Grass Valley arts and plan a night around it.
stakeholder_use: Gives reviewers an obvious institutional proof point for the map.
geo_role: Central Grass Valley anchor and likely route hub.
open_questions: needs-user-review: resolve duplicate Center records before implementation.
decision_basis: Strong official arts institution, current programming, Grass Valley hub, and obvious public/stakeholder legibility.

name: North Star House
anchor_hook: A Julia Morgan-designed historic house that connects Grass Valley's mining history, architecture, restoration, and civic imagination.
why_it_matters: It gives the map a heritage/design anchor that is visually memorable and different from a venue listing. It helps connect arts, history, architecture, and place stewardship.
image_status: real existing V1 image; verify asset path before implementation
photo_wish: Exterior image that makes the architecture immediately recognizable.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official North Star House site; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data; drafted-from-current-data: docs/CULTURAL-ANCHOR-SET-PROPOSAL.md
path_membership: Historic Grass Valley; architecture/design path; civic heritage path
muse_connection: Possible MUSE adjacency through design/heritage framing; no strong direct MUSE claim yet.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward heritage/stewardship connection; verify before making prominent claims.
events_connection: Event/site-rental relevance exists but should not dominate the card.
visitor_use: Understand an iconic Grass Valley place beyond ordinary venue categories.
stakeholder_use: Shows the map can make heritage assets legible without reducing them to tourism stops.
open_questions: needs-user-review: decide whether heritage/design anchors belong beside active arts venues in V1.
demo_decision: Promote as a primary heritage/design anchor.
decision_basis: Pairs well with Empire Mine and gives the demo preservation, architecture, and civic-history depth.

name: Nevada City Winery
anchor_hook: A downtown winery and gallery stop that can connect art viewing, local gathering, and an evening Nevada City path.
why_it_matters: It is useful because it behaves like a cultural connector: less formal than an institution, but strong for public route-making, evening use, and local texture.
image_status: real existing V1 image; verify asset path before implementation
photo_wish: Interior/gallery or storefront image that shows art and place, not only wine bottles.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official Nevada City Winery site; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data; drafted-from-current-data: docs/CULTURAL-ANCHOR-SET-PROPOSAL.md
path_membership: Nevada City evening path; downtown gallery/wine path
muse_connection: Possible MUSE/gallery adjacency; verify before claiming a direct MUSE tie.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward downtown-economy/culture connection; keep modest without direct evidence.
events_connection: Likely tastings, music, exhibitions, or public events; verify current calendar before app copy.
visitor_use: Build a low-friction evening path around art, wine, and downtown walking.
stakeholder_use: Shows that cultural discovery includes hybrid gathering places, not only formal venues.
geo_role: Downtown Nevada City connector between arts, food, and walking routes.
open_questions: needs-user-review: decide whether it should be a top-level anchor or a path stop.
demo_decision: Use as a marquee stop, not a primary anchor.
decision_basis: Useful for evening/experience routes, but less clean as a top cultural anchor than theatre/gallery/institutional sites.

name: Empire Mine
anchor_hook: A major historic landscape that gives visitors a concrete way to understand the region's mining history, grounds, architecture, and public memory.
why_it_matters: It should be framed carefully: not an arts venue, but a major orientation point for the cultural landscape that shaped Grass Valley and Nevada County identity.
image_status: real existing V1 image; verify asset path before implementation
photo_wish: Recognizable grounds, mine yard, or historic architecture image.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: California State Parks Empire Mine page; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data; drafted-from-current-data: docs/CULTURAL-ANCHOR-SET-PROPOSAL.md
path_membership: Historic landscape path; Grass Valley orientation path
muse_connection: No direct MUSE claim recommended unless a specific exhibit/article is found.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward context as heritage/cultural landscape; do not overclaim.
events_connection: Public tours/programming may be relevant; verify official park programming if used.
visitor_use: Understand the place history behind the region before or after arts stops.
stakeholder_use: Shows the prototype can orient visitors in the broader cultural landscape.
geo_role: Large Grass Valley landmark and spatial reference point.
open_questions: needs-user-review: confirm whether this belongs in anchor tier or supporting context tier.
demo_decision: Promote as a primary cultural landscape anchor with careful copy.
decision_basis: Official state historic park and major regional orientation point; frame as heritage landscape, not an arts venue.

name: Hirschman Trail / Hirschman's Pond
anchor_hook: A pond-and-trail anchor that lets the map connect cultural discovery to walking, public space, and the landscape around Nevada City.
why_it_matters: It should be framed as an outdoor place anchor, not an arts venue. Its value is wayfinding, pacing, landscape context, and possible public-art/nature-path connection.
photo_wish: Pond/trail image with enough place specificity to orient a visitor.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: Bear Yuba Land Trust Hirschman Trail page; evidence-backed: Nevada City trails/open-space page; evidence-backed: docs/anchor-cards/anchor-photo-candidates.csv; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data
path_membership: Outdoor pause path; Nevada City walking path; cultural landscape path
muse_connection: Possible MUSE/public-art/nature adjacency; verify any current installation or article before claiming.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward access/wellbeing/place connection; keep modest without direct evidence.
events_connection: Trail programming not central; do not make event claims unless sourced.
visitor_use: Add a walking/outdoor reset to a culture route and understand nearby landscape context.
stakeholder_use: Shows that the map can support mixed cultural itineraries, not only venue-hopping.
geo_role: Outdoor edge anchor for Nevada City routes.
open_questions: needs-user-review: confirm exact place ID and whether any public-art installation claim is current.
demo_decision: Use as a trail/outdoor layer stop, not a primary anchor.
decision_basis: Strong for nature/history interface and route pacing, but not a central cultural institution.

name: Nevada Theatre
anchor_hook: A historic Nevada City theatre that connects live performance, local history, and downtown cultural identity.
why_it_matters: It adds a strong Nevada City performing-arts counterweight to The Center and gives the prototype a clear night-out/history anchor.
image_status: real existing V1 image; verify asset path before implementation
photo_wish: Exterior marquee or interior performance image; exterior preferred for wayfinding.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official Nevada Theatre site; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data; drafted-from-current-data: docs/CULTURAL-ANCHOR-SET-PROPOSAL.md
path_membership: Nevada City night-out path; historic theatre path
muse_connection: Possible MUSE/performance-history adjacency; needs stronger direct source before prominent claim.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward performing-arts infrastructure connection; verify if making strategic claim.
events_connection: Strong events/performance relevance; use official schedule if wiring live events.
visitor_use: Find live performance and understand Nevada City's theatre history.
stakeholder_use: Shows historic performance infrastructure in the Nevada City side of the prototype.
open_questions: needs-user-review: find stronger Arts Council/MUSE source if this stays highly prominent.
demo_decision: Promote as a primary historic performance anchor.
decision_basis: Highly legible historic theatre and active cultural venue; balances The Center on the Nevada City side.

name: Art Works Gallery
anchor_hook: A downtown Grass Valley artist cooperative that makes local makers and gallery browsing visible in the discovery map.
why_it_matters: It is valuable because it represents individual working artists and a walkable gallery experience, not just large institutions.
photo_wish: Gallery/storefront/interior image that shows local artwork and the cooperative feel.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official Art Works Gallery site; evidence-backed: docs/anchor-cards/anchor-photo-candidates.csv; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data
path_membership: Grass Valley gallery walk; local makers path
muse_connection: Likely MUSE/gallery adjacency; verify a direct MUSE listing or article before claiming.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward small-arts-business/local-artist connection; keep modest without direct evidence.
events_connection: Possible exhibitions or artist features; verify before app copy.
visitor_use: Browse locally made work and add a gallery stop to a downtown route.
stakeholder_use: Shows that the prototype includes smaller artist-facing spaces, not only anchor institutions.
geo_role: Walkable downtown Grass Valley gallery node.
open_questions: needs-user-review: decide whether this is an anchor or a high-value path stop if evidence stays thin.
demo_decision: Promote as the visual-arts/local-artist economy anchor.
decision_basis: Best compact-demo choice for representing working local artists and downtown gallery culture.

name: ASiF Studios
anchor_hook: A hands-on studio and arts education space that shows the making side of Grass Valley's creative ecosystem.
why_it_matters: It is one of the best anchors for showing process, classes, studios, and working artists. It complements performance and gallery anchors with active making.
photo_wish: Active studio/class image with people making work; avoid a static empty-room shot.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official ASiF Studios site; evidence-backed: docs/anchor-cards/anchor-photo-candidates.csv; drafted-from-current-data: V1 places data
path_membership: Working artists path; classes/workshops path; makers path
muse_connection: Possible MUSE/studio arts adjacency; verify direct listing/article before claiming.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward education/workforce/community arts connection; keep modest without direct evidence.
events_connection: Strong class/workshop relevance; use official programming pages if wiring events.
visitor_use: Find hands-on classes, studios, and local artist process.
stakeholder_use: Shows reviewers the map can represent cultural production, not only consumption.
open_questions: needs-user-review: decide whether it is a top-level anchor or route stop if evidence remains mostly official-site based.
demo_decision: Use as an important studios/classes stop, not a top pin.
decision_basis: Strong production-space story, but more specialized than the top demo anchors.

name: The Curious Forge
anchor_hook: A community makerspace that gives the prototype a clear anchor for hands-on creative technology, craft, workshops, and shared tools.
why_it_matters: It is the approved replacement for Miners Foundry in this pass because it better represents active making and contemporary creative infrastructure for the GVNC prototype scope.
image_status: real existing V1 image; verify asset path before implementation
photo_wish: Workshop image with tools, people, or active making; avoid generic logo-only imagery.
evidence_sources: evidence-backed: official Curious Forge site; drafted-from-current-data: user-approved substitution; drafted-from-current-data: docs/CULTURAL-ANCHOR-SET-PROPOSAL.md follow-up decision
path_membership: Maker path; workshops/classes path; creative technology path
muse_connection: Possible MUSE/maker-community adjacency; verify before direct claim.
culture_forward_connection: Possible Culture Forward creative workforce/learning/community infrastructure connection; keep modest until sourced.
events_connection: Strong workshop/class relevance; verify current class calendar before app copy.
visitor_use: Discover a place to make, learn, repair, prototype, or take a workshop.
stakeholder_use: Shows reviewers that the prototype can include contemporary maker culture, not just heritage or performance.
open_questions: needs-user-review: confirm whether Curious Forge fully replaces Miners Foundry for V1 or simply outranks it in this scope.
demo_decision: Promote if the demo needs a future-facing maker/creative-technology pillar; otherwise P1 route stop.
decision_basis: User-approved Miners Foundry replacement and strong maker infrastructure, but slightly less externally legible than Tier 1 civic/arts landmarks.
